bird.name: Sedge wren
bird.summary: The sedge wren (Cistothorus stellaris) is a small and secretive passerine bird in the family Troglodytidae. It is widely distributed in Nor…

relations.subject: Sedge wren
relations.evidence: It is often found in wet grasslands and meadows where it nests in the tall grasses and sedges and feeds on insects
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Sedge wren
relations.evidence: During the breeding season, sedge wrens generally occupy meadows and wet grasslands. They can however live in wetter areas such as marshes and drier habitat such as dry prairies. They prefer areas with dense and tall grasses and sedges to build their nests. During winter, migratory sedge wrens can be found in a variety of habitat as long as there are sufficient insects to eat.{{cite journal|last1=Brooks | first1=Matthew E.| last2=Stouffer | first2=Philip C
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: Sedge wren
relations.evidence: During the breeding season, sedge wrens generally occupy meadows and wet grasslands. They can however live in wetter areas such as marshes and drier habitat such as dry prairies. They prefer areas with dense and tall grasses and sedges to build their nests. During winter, migratory sedge wrens can be found in a variety of habitat as long as there are sufficient insects to eat.{{cite journal|last1=Brooks | first1=Matthew E.| last2=Stouffer | first2=Philip C
relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Sedge wren
relations.evidence: | year=2011 | title=Interspecific variation in habitat preferences of grassland birds wintering in southern pine savannas | journal=Wilson Journal of Ornithology | volume=123 | issue=1 | pages=65–75 | jstor=23033484 | doi=10.1676/10-039.1 | s2cid=5253039}} For example, they can be found in pine savannas, dry prairies, meadows, marshes, and bogs
